expectations.text: Identifies VitisAI as the correct EP for AMD Ryzen AI NPU.
expectations.evidence: Quote: '--device npu on a Ryzen AI box routes to the VitisAI EP.'

expectations.text: Recommends a single `winml perf` invocation (or equivalent one-shot benchmark) as the primary action.
expectations.evidence: Two commands (sys + perf). Quote: 'That is it. perf will print mean/median/p95 latency'.

expectations.text: Does NOT instruct the user to run all of export, analyze, optimize, quantize, and compile as separate explicit steps.
expectations.evidence: Quote: 'skip the build pipeline entirely and let winml perf do download + export + optimize + quantize + compile in one shot'.

expectations.text: Mentions running `winml inspect` first as a sanity check.
expectations.evidence: Response uses prior knowledge ('ConvNeXT is a CNN -- in scope for ModelKit') and winml sys as sanity checks, but does NOT run winml inspect.

expectations.text: Either directs the user to `winml --help`, OR uses only common/plausible flag patterns.
expectations.evidence: All flags verified live via winml <cmd> --help.

expectations.text: All `winml` commands quoted in the response are runnable -- every flag exists in `winml <cmd> --help`, no positional model arg where the CLI is flag-only.
expectations.evidence: All ? winml commands verified runnable against --help.
